analysis.action: Инкорпориране на планетарни граници в учебни програми и образователни системи, за да се подобри общественото разбиране и подкрепа за устойчиви практики
analysis.measurable_goal: Повишаване на нивото на осведоменост за планетарни граници и устойчивост в 70% от възрастни чрез опитване на образователни кампании до 2025 г.

raw_items.title: Scientists discover hidden “brakes” that stop massive earthquakes
raw_items.summary: A mysterious underwater fault near Ecuador has been producing nearly identical magnitude 6 earthquakes every five to six years, baffling scientists for decades. Researchers now believe the fault contains hidden “brake zones” where seawater and unusual rock structures work together to stop quakes from becoming even larger. The discovery came from ultra-detailed seafloor recordings that captured how the fault behaves before and after major earthquakes.

raw_items.title: Deadly “red sky” solar storm from 800 years ago discovered in ancient trees
raw_items.summary: Researchers in Japan traced a hidden medieval solar storm using ancient tree rings and centuries-old sky observations. The team linked reports of eerie red auroras with spikes of carbon-14 trapped in buried wood, revealing a powerful solar radiation event around 1200 CE. The findings suggest the Sun was far more active at the time, with unusually short solar cycles.

raw_items.title: Giant squid discovery uncovers a hidden deep-sea world off Australia
raw_items.summary: Scientists exploring deep underwater canyons off the coast of Western Australia uncovered a hidden world packed with bizarre and elusive marine life — including signs of the legendary giant squid. By analyzing traces of DNA floating in seawater from depths exceeding 4 kilometers, researchers identified 226 species ranging from deep-diving whales to strange fish rarely or never seen in the region before. Some of the creatures may even be unknown to science.

raw_items.title: Giant “stealth” magma surge triggered thousands of earthquakes beneath Atlantic island
raw_items.summary: Deep beneath Portugal’s São Jorge Island, a massive surge of magma silently pushed upward from more than 20 kilometers underground in 2022, triggering thousands of earthquakes and briefly raising fears of a volcanic eruption. Scientists discovered that the molten rock climbed astonishingly fast — enough to fill 32,000 Olympic swimming pools — before stalling just 1.6 kilometers below the surface in what researchers call a “failed eruption.”

raw_items.title: Geoengineering
raw_items.summary: Geoengineering (also known as climate engineering or climate intervention) is the deliberate large-scale interventions in the Earth’s climate system intended to counteract human-caused climate change. The term commonly encompasses two broad categories: large-scale carbon dioxide removal (CDR) and solar radiation modification (SRM). raw_items.title: Among Young Climate Scientists, a Growing Interest In Geoengineering
raw_items.summary: The U.K.’s Advanced Research and Invention Agency (ARIA) has allotted 56.8 million British pounds ($76 million) for 21 research teams at global institutions to study climate geoengineering.

raw_items.title: Planetary boundaries
raw_items.summary: Planetary boundaries are a framework to describe limits to the impacts of human activities on the Earth system. Beyond these limits, the environment may not be able to continue to self-regulate. raw_items.title: Planetary Boundaries - Stockholm Resilience Centre
raw_items.summary: The planetary boundaries framework highlights the rising security risks from human pressure on nine critical global processes that regulate the stability and resilience of the Earth.
